{{Infobox artifact
| name = Divine Treasure
| introduced = Underlord
}}

''See also: [[Divine Treasure Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Divine Treasures are exceptionally powerful and unique objects that differ from [[natural treasures]]. While [[natural treasures]] can be found in locations like [[Night Wheel Valley]] or gathered in sets, Divine Treasures are often custom-crafted by skilled artisans, soulsmiths, or [[sacred artists]] using rare components such as the blood and cores of high-level [[Remnants]]. They can take many forms, including massive books made of light and script, suits of armor, rings, bracelets, weapons, or even specialized tools like Madra Engines and celestial crowns. Some are legendary artifacts used to symbolize status or grant authority, such as the [[Broken Crown]] or [[the Queen]]'s [[Gift]]. These treasures are frequently integrated into a user's [[soulspace]] or spirit, allowing them to manifest externally as projections—such as halos, crowns, or floating books—to augment the user's abilities. They can be powered by feeding them [[madra]] from specific [[Paths]], and they serve various functions ranging from providing near-instant transportation to enabling extreme physical regeneration or spatial manipulation. Because they are often tied to an individual's soul, they can be difficult to modify or remove. Some Divine Treasures are also passed down through lineages, requiring specific biological conditions like a [[Jade]] core to bond with descendants.}}

== Underlord ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Kiro's armor]] is a [[Divine Treasure]] kept in his soul.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c10">''Underlord'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Uncrowned ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Dreadgod corpses]] could be crafted into powerful Divine Treasures.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c15">''Uncrowned'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|A [[Divine Treasure]] was crafted by [[Soulsmiths]] from the cores and blood essences of a hundred [[Lord]]-level [[Remnants]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c19">''Uncrowned'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Divine Treasures are specialized pieces crafted by the premier artisans of the eight factions to enhance a user's combat capabilities. Within the [[Eight]]-Man [[Empire]], these objects are utilized in a network to link its members together. Examples of such items include the disc-shaped treasure held within [[Calan Archer]]'s [[soulspace]], as he possesses two such artifacts, and the dark, cracked crown projected from [[Yerin's spirit]]. While the [[Blood Shadow]] is able to recognize the inherent power of a Divine Treasure, [[Lindon]] eventually tossed his own into his [[void key]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">7</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Yerin]]'s [[Divine Treasure]] is inside her spirit and projects a dark crown with a crack down the center.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c7">''Wintersteel'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[The Eight-Man Empire]] uses a network of Divine Treasures to link its members.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c14">''Wintersteel'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Calan Archer]] has a disc-shaped [[Divine Treasure]] in his [[soulspace]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c17">''Wintersteel'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Calan Archer]] possesses two Divine Treasures.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blood Shadow]] recognizes the power of the [[Divine Treasure]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c20">''Wintersteel'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Divine Treasures are custom pieces crafted by the finest artisans of the eight factions to improve combat ability.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c23">''Wintersteel'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Lindon]] tossed the [[Divine Treasure]] into his [[void key]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c27">''Wintersteel'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Dreadgod ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Modifying a [[Divine Treasure]] is more difficult than removing a Book.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c10">''Dreadgod'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Mercy]] has a [[Divine Treasure]] in her spirit.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c23">''Dreadgod'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Waybound ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">4</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]]'s [[Divine Treasure]] resembles a silver moon orbited by rings of intricate silver script.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c4">''Waybound'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Ziel]]'s [[Grand Oath Array]] is forged by his [[Divine Treasure]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c7">''Waybound'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Calling Storms]]' reconstruction is a [[Divine Treasure]] gifted by the [[Sage of Red Faith]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c14">''Waybound'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Ziel]] can operate his [[Divine Treasure]] to some degree even without [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c16">''Waybound'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Artifacts]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle artifacts]]
